domain: Spider trichobothria (Cupiennius salei wandering spider, ~100 hairs per leg): hair lengths 100-1400 μm; each length tuned to a specific mechanical resonance frequency 40-600 Hz; full array = bank of frequency-tuned filters; phasic response to air-current deflection speed; multi-scale spatial-temporal decomposition.
specific_mechanism: Bank of varying-length hairs each acting as mechanical bandpass filter; geometric-length-determined frequency tuning; passive parallel decomposition of stimulus spectrum without active processing.
llm_application: TRICHOBOTHRIA per-head frequency-band tuning cascade: assign each MHA head h_k a FIXED positional-frequency mask m_k(t-t') = cos(2π f_k Δ) · exp(-Δ^2 / σ_k^2) with f_k = f_0 · 2^(k/H) (geometric H-octave spacing) and σ_k = c/f_k (bandwidth ∝ 1/f). Apply m_k as multiplicative bias on attention scores: score_k(t, t') = QK^T_k + log m_k(t-t'). Through the layer cascade, low-frequency heads accumulate long-range context, high-frequency heads accumulate local. Differs from (a) ALiBi linear distance bias, (b) RoPE rotary positional encoding, (c) PowerAttention DAG sparse patterns, (d) FNet Fourier mixing by combining (i) FIXED PER-HEAD geometric frequency f_k + (ii) BANDPASS cos*Gaussian mask + (iii) CASCADE integration across layers.
novelty_claim: ALiBi linear bias. RoPE rotary. FNet Fourier-mixing. Multi-scale cascaded ViT learns scales. TRICHOBOTHRIA recombines (a) PER-HEAD GEOMETRIC FREQUENCY ASSIGNMENT + (b) COS*GAUSSIAN bandpass mask + (c) LAYER CASCADE integration. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
content_words: geometric f_k frequency spacing across heads

llm_side: geometric f_k frequency spacing across heads
